coolant_temp.cold: Below 80°C — thermostat not yet open, engine in warm-up enrichment
coolant_temp.hot_warning: Above 105°C — thermostat housing, water pump, fan, or coolant level issue
coolant_temp.critical: Above 115°C — PULL OVER IMMEDIATELY. Engine damage imminent.
coolant_temp.stuck_low: Never reaches 85°C — thermostat stuck open. Poor fuel economy, P0125.

rpm.hunting: RPM oscillating ±100 RPM at idle — vacuum leak, throttle body, IAC issue
rpm.high_idle: Above 900 RPM warm — vacuum leak, throttle body carbon, sticking ISC
rpm.rough_idle: RPM drops below 600 — misfire, vacuum leak, fuel delivery issue

fuel_trims.stft_lean: Above +10% — possible vacuum leak, fuel pressure low, MAF underreporting
fuel_trims.stft_rich: Below -10% — possible purge valve stuck open, fuel pressure high, injector leak
fuel_trims.ltft_lean: Above +15% — chronic vacuum leak or failing MAF. Check both banks.
fuel_trims.ltft_rich: Below -15% — chronic rich condition. Check purge valve, fuel pressure.
fuel_trims.bank_imbalance: If Bank 1 and Bank 2 trims differ by >5%, suspect bank-specific leak
fuel_trims.both_banks_lean: Both banks lean = common vacuum leak (intake manifold, PCV, brake booster)
fuel_trims.one_bank_lean: One bank lean = bank-specific leak (valve cover gasket, runner gasket)

voltage.low_running: Below 13.5V running — alternator weak, belt slipping, diode failure
voltage.high_running: Above 14.8V — voltage regulator fault, overcharging, battery damage risk
voltage.very_low: Below 11V — battery critically low, engine may stall, power steering heavy
